## Summary

What does this PR change?

## Change type

Choose one or more:
- [ ] add
- [ ] split
- [ ] merge
- [ ] reorder
- [ ] reweight
- [ ] reinterpret
- [ ] remap
- [ ] deprecate
- [ ] promote
- [ ] retire

## Layers touched

Choose all that apply:
- [ ] schema / vocabulary
- [ ] data / nodes
- [ ] relationships / graph objects
- [ ] retrieval / rendering
- [ ] governance / lifecycle
- [ ] AI quarantine / review lane
- [ ] validation / CI
- [ ] docs / roadmap

## Why this is needed

Describe the problem or gap this PR addresses.

## Identity and lifecycle impact

- Are any immutable IDs introduced, changed, deprecated, or retired?
- Are labels changing without ID changes?
- Are any lifecycle states changing?

## Asserted vs inferred handling

- Does this PR touch asserted material, inferred/generated material, or both?
- If both, how are they kept separate?

## Validation evidence

List validation or review steps completed.

- [ ] file format / parse checks
- [ ] internal link or reference validation
- [ ] schema / contract validation
- [ ] retrieval or renderer validation
- [ ] manual review completed

## Migration notes

If this is breaking or soft-breaking, describe:
- replacement pointers
- deprecation windows
- backfill or redirect steps
- downstream files or reports needing recompute

## Codex merge notes

How should Codex compare or merge this with parallel work?

- competing branch or PR (if known):
- strongest files or patterns to keep:
- safest files to drop or supersede:
- reasons this is better, worse, or just different:

## Reviewer focus

What should reviewers inspect most carefully?
